_comment: Golden known-answer vectors for the signature dialects this package sends. 'vectors' holds the symmetric Standard Webhooks (v1) case — the canonical example published in the Standard Webhooks specification and reproduced by every official SDK, so it is an external, non-circular oracle for byte-compatibility. 'ed25519_vectors' holds the asymmetric v1a case: Ed25519 signing is deterministic (RFC 8032), so signing the same content with the same key reproduces the same signature byte for byte. 'negative_vectors' MUST FAIL to verify — an implementation that accepts everything passes every positive vector too, so a correct port has to reject these. Every key here is test material published on purpose; never use one for real traffic. See README.md in this directory for the wire format and the verification recipe.

negative_vectors.name: v1-tampered-payload
negative_vectors.reason: The signature is the spec example's, but one byte of the payload changed. A verifier must sign the body it actually received, so this cannot verify.

negative_vectors.name: v1-wrong-secret
negative_vectors.reason: The content is the spec example's, but the signature was produced under a different key. Verifying it with this secret must fail.

negative_vectors.name: v1-flipped-signature-byte
negative_vectors.reason: One character of the base64 signature differs from the spec example's. A near miss must be rejected as firmly as a wild one — compare the whole digest, in constant time.

negative_vectors.name: v1a-wrong-public-key
negative_vectors.reason: A valid Ed25519 signature from the vector above, checked against a DIFFERENT signer's public key. Only the key that signed may verify it.
